findings.fix_prompt:
Remove the "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)" button, the "Choose Your Dev Role"
modal, and all "For development purposes only" copy from the sign-in page. Only
show these when import.meta.env.DEV is true. Never on the published
lovable.app URL.
findings.title: Anyone can skip login — dev bypass on the live URL
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle Marketplace's sign-in page ships with a "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)"
button. One click opens a role picker and drops visitors into the app
with no password.
findings.why_it_matters:
We catch this on most Lovable apps that shipped before a final "remove dev UI"
pass. On a marketplace, strangers can act as hosts or buyers.

findings.fix_prompt:
Remove the "Developer Tools" / "Development Data Seeder" section from
the published homepage. Gate the Seed and Clear data buttons behind
import.meta.env.DEV only.
findings.title: "Developer Tools" panel on the homepage customers see
findings.what_we_saw:
After sign-in, Sparkle's homepage shows a "Developer Tools" card with
"Seed Sample Data" and "Clear My Data" — visible on the public URL.
findings.why_it_matters: Paying users read this as "still in beta." It undercuts every polished pixel around it.

findings.fix_prompt:
Add the privacy page and the terms page routes with basic policy content. Link both
from the footer on every page. Use [email redacted] (or your
real support email) in the contact section. Match the rest of the
site's typography and spacing.
findings.title: Privacy and Terms links go to 404
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle's footer promises legal pages, but both privacy and terms URLs
return the app's 404 screen.
findings.why_it_matters: Stripe and B2B buyers look for these before they trust you with money or data.

findings.fix_prompt:
Fix Quick Book so available slots appear, OR hide Quick Book when
no slots exist and show "No dates available yet, check back soon."
findings.title: Hero "Quick Book" CTA dead-ends — zero bookable slots
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle's main "Quick Book" button opens a modal that says "No available
slots found" and keeps "Book Now" disabled.
findings.why_it_matters: Every visitor who tries to book hits a wall — and most won't email you about it.

findings.fix_prompt:
On Sparkle's Quick Book modal, constrain height to the viewport, make the
slot list scroll inside the modal, and pin "Book Now" above the safe area
on mobile. Test on iPhone SE and 390px width.
findings.title: Booking modal breaks on mobile — CTA below the fold
findings.what_we_saw:
At phone width, Sparkle's Quick Book modal is taller than the screen. The
time picker and "Book Now" button sit below the fold with no obvious scroll.
findings.why_it_matters: Most vibe-coded launches get more than half their traffic on phones.

findings.fix_prompt:
When the review count is 0, show "New" or "No reviews yet" instead of
a numeric rating. Only show the star number once at least one real
review exists.
findings.title: Listings show 4.5 stars with (0) reviews
findings.what_we_saw: Cards display "4.5 (0)", a numeric rating with zero reviews.
findings.why_it_matters: Reads like seeded test data, which kills trust on a marketplace.

findings.fix_prompt:
Replace "Hosted by Local Host" with each experience's actual host
display name from the database. If missing, show first name + last
initial or hide the line.
findings.title: Every Sparkle listing says "Hosted by Local Host"
findings.what_we_saw:
Every Sparkle Marketplace experience card shows "Hosted by Local Host"
instead of a real host name.
findings.why_it_matters: Reads as placeholder data on a public marketplace.

passed_checks: Search, filters, and listing detail pages feel production-ready
Core branding and layout are ahead of most pre-launch vibe-coded apps
Auth and navigation work once dev-only UI is removed

share_metadata.description: Pre-launch audit. Verdict: Needs fixes before launch. 7 findings across trust, broken CTAs, mobile layout, and more.
share_metadata.title: LaunchLook audit for Sparkle Marketplace

verdict.label: Needs fixes before launch
verdict.narrative:
Sparkle Marketplace looks investor-ready in screenshots — then you click
through. Dev shortcuts are still on the live URL, legal pages 404, the hero
booking CTA fails, and mobile checkout is effectively broken. Clear the two
critical items and the booking path before you share the link publicly.
verdict.summary: Polished shell, three launch-stoppers we'd fix before you post the URL anywhere.
